Design

0) Concept

0-1) Types

  • Data Structure Design: Design custom data structures (Stack, Queue, HashMap, etc.)
  • Cache Design: LRU, LFU, Time-based cache systems
  • System Component Design: File systems, search systems, rate limiters
  • Social Network Design: Twitter, Instagram feed, following/follower systems
  • Scheduling/Booking Design: Calendar, meeting rooms, parking systems
  • Stream/Iterator Design: Data stream processing, custom iterators
  • Game Design: Tic-Tac-Toe, Snake, game boards

0-2) Pattern

Pattern 1: HashMap + LinkedList

  • Use Case: Order-sensitive operations (LRU, LFU, insertion order)
  • Examples: LRU Cache, LFU Cache, Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
  • Key Point: HashMap provides O(1) lookup, LinkedList provides O(1) ordering operations

Pattern 2: HashMap + Heap

  • Use Case: Priority-based operations, top-k problems
  • Examples: Design Twitter, Top K Frequent Elements in stream
  • Key Point: HashMap tracks data, Heap maintains priority order

Pattern 3: Trie (Prefix Tree)

  • Use Case: Autocomplete, prefix search, word validation
  • Examples: Search Autocomplete System, Add and Search Word, Design Search System
  • Key Point: Efficient prefix-based operations O(L) where L is word length

Pattern 4: OOD (Object-Oriented Design)

  • Use Case: Complex system with multiple components and interactions
  • Examples: Parking Lot, Elevator System, Library Management
  • Key Point: Focus on classes, interfaces, relationships, and SOLID principles

Pattern 5: Stream/Queue Based

  • Use Case: Real-time data processing, moving window operations
  • Examples: Moving Average, Hit Counter, Rate Limiter
  • Key Point: Deque or Queue for time-window based operations

1) General form

1-1) Basic OP

Step 1: Clarify Requirements

  • What operations need to be supported?
  • What are the time/space complexity requirements?
  • What are the edge cases? (empty input, duplicates, concurrency)
  • What is the expected scale? (single machine vs distributed)

Step 2: Choose Data Structures

  • Map key requirements to appropriate data structures
  • Consider trade-offs (time vs space, simplicity vs performance)
  • Multiple data structures often needed (HashMap + List, HashMap + Heap, etc.)

Step 3: Define Class Structure

1class DesignName:
2    def __init__(self, params):
3        # Initialize data structures
4        self.data_structure1 = {}
5        self.data_structure2 = []
6
7    def operation1(self, params):
8        # Implement operation
9        pass
10
11    def operation2(self, params):
12        # Implement operation
13        pass

Step 4: Implement Core Operations

  • Focus on the required methods
  • Maintain invariants (data consistency between structures)
  • Handle edge cases

Step 5: Optimize

  • Identify bottlenecks
  • Use appropriate data structures for O(1) operations when needed
  • Consider lazy evaluation or caching

1-2) Interview Tips

Tip 1: Ask Clarifying Questions

  • “Should this support concurrent access?” (Usually no for LC problems)
  • “What should happen if we try to get a non-existent key?”
  • “Are there any constraints on input size or range?”
  • “Do we need to support deletion/updates?”

Tip 2: Start with Simple Solution

  • Start with brute force using basic data structures
  • Explain time/space complexity
  • Then optimize based on requirements

Tip 3: Data Structure Selection

  • Need fast lookup? → HashMap/HashSet
  • Need ordering? → LinkedList, TreeMap, Heap
  • Need both? → Combine them (HashMap + LinkedList for LRU)
  • Prefix operations? → Trie
  • Range queries? → Segment Tree, Binary Indexed Tree
  • Time-based operations? → Queue/Deque with timestamps

Tip 4: Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Not maintaining consistency between multiple data structures
  • Forgetting to handle edge cases (empty, single element, duplicates)
  • Not considering time complexity of helper operations
  • Over-engineering (keep it simple if requirements allow)

Tip 5: OOD Specific Tips

  • Define clear interfaces and responsibilities
  • Use meaningful class and method names
  • Consider SOLID principles (especially Single Responsibility)
  • Think about extensibility and maintainability

1-3) Things to Notice

Notice 1: OrderedDict in Python

  • Combines HashMap and LinkedList functionality
  • move_to_end(key): O(1) operation to reorder
  • popitem(last=False): Remove first item (FIFO), last=True for LIFO
  • Perfect for LRU/LFU cache implementations

Notice 2: Double Data Structure Pattern

  • When using multiple data structures, ensure they stay synchronized
  • Example: LRU uses cache_dict (lookup) + cache_list (order)
  • Always update BOTH when adding/removing/modifying

Notice 3: Dummy Nodes for LinkedList

  • Use dummy head/tail nodes to simplify edge cases
  • Avoids null checks for head/tail operations
  • Common in LRU Cache implementations

Notice 4: Time-based Expiration

  • Use timestamp + cleanup strategy
  • Lazy cleanup: Remove expired items when accessed
  • Eager cleanup: Use heap/queue to track expiration times
  • Trade-off: Space (keeping old data) vs Time (cleanup overhead)

Notice 5: defaultdict and Counter

1from collections import defaultdict, Counter
2# Avoid key existence checks
3followers = defaultdict(set)  # Auto-creates empty set
4tweet_count = defaultdict(int)  # Auto-creates 0

1-4) Classic LC Problems by Category

Category 1: Cache Design ⭐⭐⭐

  • LC 146. LRU Cache (Medium) - HashMap + DoublyLinkedList
  • LC 460. LFU Cache (Hard) - HashMap + OrderedDict for frequency buckets
  • LC 432. All O(1) Data Structure (Hard) - HashMap + DoublyLinkedList of buckets
  • LC 1756. Design Most Recently Used Queue (Medium)

Category 2: Data Structure Design

  • LC 380. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1) (Medium) - HashMap + ArrayList
  • LC 381. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1) - Duplicates (Hard)
  • LC 211. Design Add and Search Words Data Structure (Medium) - Trie
  • LC 208. Implement Trie (Prefix Tree) (Medium)
  • LC 641. Design Circular Deque (Medium)
  • LC 622. Design Circular Queue (Medium)
  • LC 225. Implement Stack using Queues (Easy)
  • LC 232. Implement Queue using Stacks (Easy)

Category 3: Stream/Time-based Design

  • LC 346. Moving Average from Data Stream (Easy) - Queue
  • LC 362. Design Hit Counter (Medium) - Queue with timestamps
  • LC 353. Design Snake Game (Medium) - Queue + Set
  • LC 1396. Design Underground System (Medium) - HashMap
  • LC 981. Time Based Key-Value Store (Medium) - HashMap + Binary Search

Category 4: File System Design

  • LC 1166. Design File System (Medium) - HashMap for path storage
  • LC 588. Design In-Memory File System (Hard) - Trie-like nested dict structure
  • LC 1244. Design A Leaderboard (Medium) - HashMap + TreeMap

Category 5: Social Network Design

  • LC 355. Design Twitter (Medium) - HashMap + Heap for feed merging
  • LC 1603. Design Parking System (Easy) - Simple counter

Category 6: Search/Autocomplete Design

  • LC 642. Design Search Autocomplete System (Hard) - Trie + Heap
  • LC 1268. Search Suggestions System (Medium) - Trie or Sorting
  • LC 1146. Snapshot Array (Medium) - HashMap for snapshots

Category 7: Iterator Design

  • LC 284. Peeking Iterator (Medium) - Iterator wrapper with lookahead
  • LC 251. Flatten 2D Vector (Medium) - Two pointers
  • LC 341. Flatten Nested List Iterator (Medium) - Stack for DFS
  • LC 281. Zigzag Iterator (Medium) - Queue of iterators

Category 8: Rate Limiter Design

  • LC 362. Design Hit Counter (Medium) - Sliding window
  • Design Token Bucket Rate Limiter (Common interview question)
  • Design Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter (Common interview question)

Category 9: Game Design

  • LC 348. Design Tic-Tac-Toe (Medium) - Row/Col/Diagonal counters
  • LC 353. Design Snake Game (Medium) - Queue + Set
  • LC 1286. Iterator for Combination (Medium)

2) LC Example

2-1) Design File System

1# LC 1166. Design File System
2# V1
3# IDEA : dict
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/design-file-system/discuss/365925/Python-dict-solution
5class FileSystem:
6
7    def __init__(self):
8        self.d = {}
9
10    def createPath(self, path: str, value: int) -> bool:
11        if path in self.d: return False
12        if len(path) == 1: return False
13        idx = len(path) - 1
14        while path[idx] != '/': idx -= 1
15        if idx == 0 or path[:idx] in self.d: 
16            self.d[path] = value
17            return True
18        return False
19        
20    def get(self, path: str) -> int:
21        return self.d.get(path, -1)

2-2) Design In-Memory File System

1# LC 588. Design In-Memory File System
2
3# V0
4# IDEA : Dict
5class FileSystem(object):
6
7    def __init__(self):
8        """
9        NOTE !!! we init root as below structure
10        """
11        self.root = {'dirs' : {}, 'files': {}}
12
13    def ls(self, path):
14        """
15        :type path: str
16        :rtype: List[str]
17        """
18        node, type = self.getExistedNode(path)
19        if type == 'dir':
20            return sorted(node['dirs'].keys() + node['files'].keys())
21        return [path.split('/')[-1]]
22
23    def mkdir(self, path):
24        """
25        :type path: str
26        :rtype: void
27        """
28        node = self.root
29        #for dir in filter(len, path.split('/')):
30        for dir in [ x for x in path.split('/') if len(x) > 0 ]:
31            if dir not in node['dirs']:
32                node['dirs'][dir] = {'dirs' : {}, 'files': {}}
33            node = node['dirs'][dir]
34
35    def addContentToFile(self, filePath, content):
36        """
37        :type filePath: str
38        :type content: str
39        :rtype: void
40        """
41        dirs = filePath.split('/')
42        path, file = '/'.join(dirs[:-1]), dirs[-1]
43        self.mkdir(path)
44        node, type = self.getExistedNode(path)
45        if file not in node['files']:
46            node['files'][file] = ''
47        node['files'][file] += content
48
49    def readContentFromFile(self, filePath):
50        """
51        :type filePath: str
52        :rtype: str
53        """
54        dirs = filePath.split('/')
55        path, file = '/'.join(dirs[:-1]), dirs[-1]
56        node, type = self.getExistedNode(path)
57        return node['files'][file]
58        
59    def getExistedNode(self, path):
60        """
61        :type path: str
62        :rtype: str
63        """
64        node = self.root
65
66        # method 1) : filter
67        # https://www.runoob.com/python/python-func-filter.html
68        #print ("*** path = " + str(path))
69        #print ("*** filter(len, path.split('/') = " + str(filter(len, path.split('/'))))
70        #for dir in filter(len, path.split('/')): # filter out path.split('/') outcome which with len > 0
71
72        # method 2) list comprehension with condition
73        for dir in [ x for x in path.split('/') if len(x) > 0 ]:
74            if dir in node['dirs']: 
75                node = node['dirs'][dir]
76            else:
77                return node, 'file'
78        return node, 'dir'

2-3) LRU Cache

1# LC 146 LRU Cache (Least Recently Used (LRU) cache)
2# V0
3# IDEA : ARRAY + LRU (implement LRU via array)
4class LRUCache(object):
5
6    def __init__(self, capacity):
7        self.capacity = capacity
8        self._cache = []   
9        self._cache_look_up = {}
10
11    def get(self, key):
12        if key not in self._cache_look_up:
13            return -1
14
15        self._cache.remove(key)
16        self._cache.append(key)
17
18        return self._cache_look_up[key]
19
20    def put(self, key, value):
21        # case 1) key in cache
22        if key in self._cache_look_up:
23            self._cache_look_up[key] = value
24
25            """
26            NOTE !!! below trick
27                In [14]: x = [1,2,3]
28                In [15]: x.remove(2)
29                In [16]: x
30                Out[16]: [1, 3]
31                In [17]: x.append(2)
32                In [18]: x
33                Out[18]: [1, 3, 2]
34            """
35            
36            self._cache.remove(key)
37            self._cache.append(key)
38            return
39
40        # case 2) key NOT in cache
41        else:
42            # case 2-1) len(cache) == capacity -> need to clear cache with LRU
43            if len(self._cache) == self.capacity:
44                del_key = self._cache[0]
45                self._cache = self._cache[1:]
46                del self._cache_look_up[del_key]
47
48            # case 2-2) len(cache) < capacity
49            self._cache.append(key)
50            self._cache_look_up[key] = value
51
52# V1
53# IDEA : Ordered dictionary
54# https://leetcode.com/problems/lru-cache/solution/
55# IDEA : 
56#       -> There is a structure called ordered dictionary, it combines behind both hashmap and linked list. 
57#       -> In Python this structure is called OrderedDict 
58#       -> and in Java LinkedHashMap.
59from collections import OrderedDict
60class LRUCache(OrderedDict):
61
62    def __init__(self, capacity):
63        """
64        :type capacity: int
65        """
66        self.capacity = capacity
67
68    def get(self, key):
69        """
70        :type key: int
71        :rtype: int
72        """
73        if key not in self:
74            return - 1
75        
76        self.move_to_end(key)
77        return self[key]
78
79    def put(self, key, value):
80        """
81        :type key: int
82        :type value: int
83        :rtype: void
84        """
85        if key in self:
86            self.move_to_end(key)
87        self[key] = value
88        if len(self) > self.capacity:
89            self.popitem(last = False)

2-4) LFU Cache

1# LC 460. LFU Cache
2# V0
3from collections import OrderedDict
4class Node:
5    def __init__(self, key, val, count):
6        self.key=key
7        self.val=val
8        self.count=count
9class LFUCache:
10    
11    def __init__(self, capacity):
12        """
13        :type capacity: int
14        """
15        self.capacity=capacity
16        self.key_node={}
17        self.count_node={}
18        self.minV=None
19    def get(self, key):
20        """
21        :type key: int
22        :rtype: int
23        """
24        if not key in self.key_node:  return -1 
25        node = self.key_node[key]
26        del self.count_node[node.count][key]
27        if not self.count_node[node.count]:
28            del self.count_node[node.count] 
29        node.count+=1
30        if not node.count in self.count_node:
31            self.count_node[node.count]=OrderedDict()
32        
33        self.count_node[node.count][key]=node
34        
35        if not self.minV in self.count_node:
36            self.minV+=1
37        return node.val
38    def put(self, key, value):
39        """
40        :type key: int
41        :type value: int
42        :rtype: void
43        """
44        # if element exists, -> update value and count + 1 
45        if self.capacity==0: return None
46        if key in self.key_node:
47            self.key_node[key].val=value
48            self.get(key)
49        else:
50            if len(self.key_node) == self.capacity:
51                item=self.count_node[self.minV].popitem(last=False)
52                del self.key_node[item[0]]
53            node=Node(key,value,1)
54            self.key_node[key]=node
55            if not 1 in self.count_node:
56                self.count_node[1]=OrderedDict()
57            
58            self.count_node[1][key]=node
59            self.minV=1
60

2-5) Design Twitter

1# LC 355  Design Twitter
2# V0
3# https://github.com/labuladong/fucking-algorithm/blob/master/%E6%95%B0%E6%8D%AE%E7%BB%93%E6%9E%84%E7%B3%BB%E5%88%97/%E8%AE%BE%E8%AE%A1Twitter.md
4from collections import defaultdict
5from heapq import merge
6class Twitter(object):
7    
8    def __init__(self):
9        self.follower_followees_map = defaultdict(set)
10        self.user_tweets_map = defaultdict(list)
11        self.time_stamp = 0
12
13    def postTweet(self, userId, tweetId):
14        self.user_tweets_map[userId].append((self.time_stamp, tweetId))
15        self.time_stamp -= 1
16
17    def getNewsFeed(self, userId):
18        # get the followees list
19        followees = self.follower_followees_map[userId]
20        # add userId as well, since he/she can also see his/her post in the timeline
21        followees.add(userId)
22        
23        # reversed(.) returns a listreverseiterator, so the complexity is O(1) not O(n)
24        candidate_tweets = [reversed(self.user_tweets_map[u]) for u in followees]
25
26        tweets = []
27        """
28        python starred expression :
29        -> will extend Iterable Unpacking
30        example 1 : *candidate_tweets
31        exmaple 2 : a, *b, c = range(5)
32        ref :
33        https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3132/
34        https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_41521681/article/details/103528136
35        http://swaywang.blogspot.com/2012/01/pythonstarred-expression.html
36        python_trick.html
37        """
38        # complexity is 10*log(n), n is twitter's user number in worst case
39        for t in merge(*candidate_tweets):
40            tweets.append(t[1])
41            if len(tweets) == 10:
42                break
43        return tweets
44
45    def follow(self, followerId, followeeId):
46        self.follower_followees_map[followerId].add(followeeId)
47
48    def unfollow(self, followerId, followeeId):
49        self.follower_followees_map[followerId].discard(followeeId)

2-6) Design Log Storage System

1# LC 635 Design Log Storage System

2-7) Design Search Autocomplete System

1# LC 642 Design Search Autocomplete System
2# V1
3# IDEA : DICT TRIE
4# http://bookshadow.com/weblog/2017/07/16/leetcode-design-search-autocomplete-system/
5class TrieNode:
6    def __init__(self):
7        self.children = dict()
8        self.sentences = set()
9
10class AutocompleteSystem(object):
11
12    def __init__(self, sentences, times):
13        """
14        :type sentences: List[str]
15        :type times: List[int]
16        """
17        self.buffer = ''
18        self.stimes = collections.defaultdict(int)
19        self.trie = TrieNode()
20        for s, t in zip(sentences, times):
21            self.stimes[s] = t
22            self.addSentence(s)
23        self.tnode = self.trie
24
25    def input(self, c):
26        """
27        :type c: str
28        :rtype: List[str]
29        """
30        ans = []
31        if c != '#':
32            self.buffer += c
33            if self.tnode: self.tnode = self.tnode.children.get(c)
34            if self.tnode: ans = sorted(self.tnode.sentences, key=lambda x: (-self.stimes[x], x))[:3]
35        else:
36            self.stimes[self.buffer] += 1
37            self.addSentence(self.buffer)
38            self.buffer = ''
39            self.tnode = self.trie
40        return ans
41
42    def addSentence(self, sentence):
43        node = self.trie
44        for letter in sentence:
45            child = node.children.get(letter)
46            if child is None:
47                child = TrieNode()
48                node.children[letter] = child
49            node = child
50            child.sentences.add(sentence)

2-8) Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)

1# LC 380. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
2# V0
3# IDEA: HashMap + ArrayList
4# - HashMap: stores value -> index mapping for O(1) lookup
5# - ArrayList: stores actual values for O(1) random access
6import random
7class RandomizedSet:
8
9    def __init__(self):
10        self.dict = {}  # value -> index in list
11        self.list = []  # stores values
12
13    def insert(self, val: int) -> bool:
14        if val in self.dict:
15            return False
16        self.dict[val] = len(self.list)
17        self.list.append(val)
18        return True
19
20    def remove(self, val: int) -> bool:
21        if val not in self.dict:
22            return False
23        # Move last element to the position of element to delete
24        last_element = self.list[-1]
25        idx = self.dict[val]
26        self.list[idx] = last_element
27        self.dict[last_element] = idx
28        # Remove last element
29        self.list.pop()
30        del self.dict[val]
31        return True
32
33    def getRandom(self) -> int:
34        return random.choice(self.list)

2-9) Design Hit Counter

1# LC 362. Design Hit Counter
2# V0
3# IDEA: Queue with timestamps (sliding window)
4from collections import deque
5class HitCounter:
6
7    def __init__(self):
8        self.hits = deque()  # stores timestamps
9
10    def hit(self, timestamp: int) -> None:
11        self.hits.append(timestamp)
12
13    def getHits(self, timestamp: int) -> int:
14        # Remove hits older than 300 seconds
15        while self.hits and timestamp - self.hits[0] >= 300:
16            self.hits.popleft()
17        return len(self.hits)
18
19# V1
20# IDEA: Array with buckets (optimized for multiple hits at same timestamp)
21class HitCounter:
22
23    def __init__(self):
24        self.times = [0] * 300
25        self.hits = [0] * 300
26
27    def hit(self, timestamp: int) -> None:
28        idx = timestamp % 300
29        if self.times[idx] != timestamp:
30            self.times[idx] = timestamp
31            self.hits[idx] = 1
32        else:
33            self.hits[idx] += 1
34
35    def getHits(self, timestamp: int) -> int:
36        total = 0
37        for i in range(300):
38            if timestamp - self.times[i] < 300:
39                total += self.hits[i]
40        return total

2-10) Design Tic-Tac-Toe

1# LC 348. Design Tic-Tac-Toe
2# V0
3# IDEA: Track row/col/diagonal sums
4# - Each player has a unique value (+1 for player1, -1 for player2)
5# - Win when any row/col/diagonal sum equals n or -n
6class TicTacToe:
7
8    def __init__(self, n: int):
9        self.n = n
10        self.rows = [0] * n
11        self.cols = [0] * n
12        self.diagonal = 0
13        self.anti_diagonal = 0
14
15    def move(self, row: int, col: int, player: int) -> int:
16        # player 1 -> +1, player 2 -> -1
17        value = 1 if player == 1 else -1
18
19        self.rows[row] += value
20        self.cols[col] += value
21
22        if row == col:
23            self.diagonal += value
24
25        if row + col == self.n - 1:
26            self.anti_diagonal += value
27
28        # Check win condition
29        if (abs(self.rows[row]) == self.n or
30            abs(self.cols[col]) == self.n or
31            abs(self.diagonal) == self.n or
32            abs(self.anti_diagonal) == self.n):
33            return player
34
35        return 0

2-11) Design Underground System

1# LC 1396. Design Underground System
2# V0
3# IDEA: Two HashMaps
4# - checkInMap: id -> (stationName, time)
5# - travelMap: (start, end) -> [total_time, count]
6from collections import defaultdict
7class UndergroundSystem:
8
9    def __init__(self):
10        self.check_in = {}  # id -> (station, time)
11        self.travel = defaultdict(lambda: [0, 0])  # (start, end) -> [total_time, count]
12
13    def checkIn(self, id: int, stationName: str, t: int) -> None:
14        self.check_in[id] = (stationName, t)
15
16    def checkOut(self, id: int, stationName: str, t: int) -> None:
17        start_station, start_time = self.check_in[id]
18        route = (start_station, stationName)
19        self.travel[route][0] += t - start_time
20        self.travel[route][1] += 1
21        del self.check_in[id]
22
23    def getAverageTime(self, startStation: str, endStation: str) -> float:
24        total_time, count = self.travel[(startStation, endStation)]
25        return total_time / count

2-12) Design Snake Game

1# LC 353. Design Snake Game
2# V0
3# IDEA: Queue for snake body + Set for fast collision check
4from collections import deque
5class SnakeGame:
6
7    def __init__(self, width: int, height: int, food: List[List[int]]):
8        self.width = width
9        self.height = height
10        self.food = deque(food)
11        self.snake = deque([(0, 0)])  # snake body positions
12        self.snake_set = {(0, 0)}  # for O(1) collision check
13        self.score = 0
14
15    def move(self, direction: str) -> int:
16        # Calculate new head position
17        head_r, head_c = self.snake[0]
18
19        if direction == "U":
20            new_r, new_c = head_r - 1, head_c
21        elif direction == "D":
22            new_r, new_c = head_r + 1, head_c
23        elif direction == "L":
24            new_r, new_c = head_r, head_c - 1
25        else:  # "R"
26            new_r, new_c = head_r, head_c + 1
27
28        # Check boundary
29        if new_r < 0 or new_r >= self.height or new_c < 0 or new_c >= self.width:
30            return -1
31
32        # Check if eating food
33        if self.food and [new_r, new_c] == self.food[0]:
34            self.food.popleft()
35            self.score += 1
36        else:
37            # Remove tail if not eating
38            tail = self.snake.pop()
39            self.snake_set.remove(tail)
40
41        # Check self-collision (after tail removal)
42        if (new_r, new_c) in self.snake_set:
43            return -1
44
45        # Add new head
46        self.snake.appendleft((new_r, new_c))
47        self.snake_set.add((new_r, new_c))
48
49        return self.score

2-13) Time Based Key-Value Store

1# LC 981. Time Based Key-Value Store
2# V0
3# IDEA: HashMap + Binary Search
4# - HashMap: key -> list of (timestamp, value) pairs
5# - List is sorted by timestamp, use binary search to find largest timestamp <= target
6from collections import defaultdict
7import bisect
8
9class TimeMap:
10
11    def __init__(self):
12        self.store = defaultdict(list)  # key -> [(timestamp, value), ...]
13
14    def set(self, key: str, value: str, timestamp: int) -> None:
15        self.store[key].append((timestamp, value))
16
17    def get(self, key: str, timestamp: int) -> str:
18        if key not in self.store:
19            return ""
20
21        values = self.store[key]
22        # Binary search for largest timestamp <= target
23        idx = bisect.bisect_right(values, (timestamp, chr(127)))
24
25        return values[idx - 1][1] if idx > 0 else ""
26
27# V1 - Manual Binary Search
28class TimeMap:
29
30    def __init__(self):
31        self.store = defaultdict(list)
32
33    def set(self, key: str, value: str, timestamp: int) -> None:
34        self.store[key].append((timestamp, value))
35
36    def get(self, key: str, timestamp: int) -> str:
37        if key not in self.store:
38            return ""
39
40        values = self.store[key]
41        left, right = 0, len(values) - 1
42        result = ""
43
44        while left <= right:
45            mid = (left + right) // 2
46            if values[mid][0] <= timestamp:
47                result = values[mid][1]
48                left = mid + 1
49            else:
50                right = mid - 1
51
52        return result

2-14) Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

1# LC 211. Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
2# V0
3# IDEA: Trie with wildcard support
4class TrieNode:
5    def __init__(self):
6        self.children = {}
7        self.is_word = False
8
9class WordDictionary:
10
11    def __init__(self):
12        self.root = TrieNode()
13
14    def addWord(self, word: str) -> None:
15        node = self.root
16        for char in word:
17            if char not in node.children:
18                node.children[char] = TrieNode()
19            node = node.children[char]
20        node.is_word = True
21
22    def search(self, word: str) -> bool:
23        return self.search_helper(word, 0, self.root)
24
25    def search_helper(self, word: str, index: int, node: TrieNode) -> bool:
26        if index == len(word):
27            return node.is_word
28
29        char = word[index]
30
31        if char == '.':
32            # Try all possible children
33            for child in node.children.values():
34                if self.search_helper(word, index + 1, child):
35                    return True
36            return False
37        else:
38            if char not in node.children:
39                return False
40            return self.search_helper(word, index + 1, node.children[char])

2-15) All O(1) Data Structure

1# LC 432. All O`one Data Structure
2# V0
3# IDEA: HashMap + Doubly Linked List of Buckets
4# - Each bucket contains all keys with the same count
5# - HashMap: key -> bucket node
6# - Doubly Linked List: ordered buckets by count
7class Node:
8    def __init__(self, count):
9        self.count = count
10        self.keys = set()
11        self.prev = None
12        self.next = None
13
14class AllOne:
15
16    def __init__(self):
17        self.key_counter = {}  # key -> count
18        self.count_node = {}   # count -> Node
19        self.head = Node(0)    # dummy head
20        self.tail = Node(0)    # dummy tail
21        self.head.next = self.tail
22        self.tail.prev = self.head
23
24    def inc(self, key: str) -> None:
25        if key in self.key_counter:
26            count = self.key_counter[key]
27            self.key_counter[key] = count + 1
28            cur_node = self.count_node[count]
29
30            # Remove key from current count bucket
31            cur_node.keys.remove(key)
32
33            # Get or create next count bucket
34            if count + 1 not in self.count_node:
35                new_node = Node(count + 1)
36                self.count_node[count + 1] = new_node
37                self._insert_after(cur_node, new_node)
38
39            self.count_node[count + 1].keys.add(key)
40
41            # Remove current bucket if empty
42            if not cur_node.keys:
43                self._remove_node(cur_node)
44                del self.count_node[count]
45        else:
46            self.key_counter[key] = 1
47            if 1 not in self.count_node:
48                new_node = Node(1)
49                self.count_node[1] = new_node
50                self._insert_after(self.head, new_node)
51            self.count_node[1].keys.add(key)
52
53    def dec(self, key: str) -> None:
54        count = self.key_counter[key]
55        cur_node = self.count_node[count]
56        cur_node.keys.remove(key)
57
58        if count == 1:
59            del self.key_counter[key]
60        else:
61            self.key_counter[key] = count - 1
62            if count - 1 not in self.count_node:
63                new_node = Node(count - 1)
64                self.count_node[count - 1] = new_node
65                self._insert_before(cur_node, new_node)
66            self.count_node[count - 1].keys.add(key)
67
68        if not cur_node.keys:
69            self._remove_node(cur_node)
70            del self.count_node[count]
71
72    def getMaxKey(self) -> str:
73        if self.tail.prev == self.head:
74            return ""
75        return next(iter(self.tail.prev.keys))
76
77    def getMinKey(self) -> str:
78        if self.head.next == self.tail:
79            return ""
80        return next(iter(self.head.next.keys))
81
82    def _insert_after(self, node, new_node):
83        new_node.prev = node
84        new_node.next = node.next
85        node.next.prev = new_node
86        node.next = new_node
87
88    def _insert_before(self, node, new_node):
89        new_node.next = node
90        new_node.prev = node.prev
91        node.prev.next = new_node
92        node.prev = new_node
93
94    def _remove_node(self, node):
95        node.prev.next = node.next
96        node.next.prev = node.prev

3) System Design Coding Patterns

3-1) Consistent Hashing

Concept:

  • Distribute data/load across nodes in a scalable way
  • Minimize rehashing when nodes are added/removed
  • Used in: Distributed caching, load balancing, CDNs
  • Key Benefit: Only k/n keys need remapping when adding/removing nodes (vs all keys)

Virtual Nodes:

  • Each physical node has multiple virtual nodes on the ring
  • Better load distribution and fault tolerance
  • Typical: 100-200 virtual nodes per physical node

Implementation:

1# V0 - Consistent Hashing with Virtual Nodes
2import hashlib
3import bisect
4from typing import List, Optional
5
6class ConsistentHashing:
7    """
8    Consistent Hashing Implementation with Virtual Nodes
9
10    Time Complexity:
11    - add_node: O(V log N) where V = virtual_nodes, N = total nodes
12    - remove_node: O(V log N)
13    - get_node: O(log N) binary search
14
15    Space: O(V × P) where P = physical nodes
16    """
17
18    def __init__(self, virtual_nodes: int = 150):
19        """
20        virtual_nodes: number of virtual nodes per physical node
21        """
22        self.virtual_nodes = virtual_nodes
23        self.ring = []  # Sorted list of (hash, node_id)
24        self.nodes = set()  # Physical nodes
25
26    def _hash(self, key: str) -> int:
27        """Generate hash value for key"""
28        return int(hashlib.md5(key.encode()).hexdigest(), 16)
29
30    def add_node(self, node_id: str) -> None:
31        """
32        Add physical node with virtual nodes
33        Time: O(V log N)
34        """
35        if node_id in self.nodes:
36            return
37
38        self.nodes.add(node_id)
39
40        # Add virtual nodes to ring
41        for i in range(self.virtual_nodes):
42            virtual_key = f"{node_id}:vnode{i}"
43            hash_value = self._hash(virtual_key)
44            bisect.insort(self.ring, (hash_value, node_id))
45
46    def remove_node(self, node_id: str) -> None:
47        """
48        Remove physical node and its virtual nodes
49        Time: O(V × N) for removal
50        """
51        if node_id not in self.nodes:
52            return
53
54        self.nodes.remove(node_id)
55
56        # Remove all virtual nodes
57        self.ring = [(h, nid) for h, nid in self.ring if nid != node_id]
58
59    def get_node(self, key: str) -> Optional[str]:
60        """
61        Find node responsible for key
62        Time: O(log N) - binary search on ring
63        """
64        if not self.ring:
65            return None
66
67        hash_value = self._hash(key)
68
69        # Binary search for first node >= hash_value
70        idx = bisect.bisect_right(self.ring, (hash_value, ''))
71
72        # Wrap around if at end
73        if idx == len(self.ring):
74            idx = 0
75
76        return self.ring[idx][1]
77
78    def get_nodes(self, key: str, count: int = 1) -> List[str]:
79        """
80        Get multiple nodes for replication
81        Time: O(log N + count)
82        """
83        if not self.ring or count <= 0:
84            return []
85
86        hash_value = self._hash(key)
87        idx = bisect.bisect_right(self.ring, (hash_value, ''))
88
89        result = []
90        seen = set()
91
92        # Traverse ring clockwise
93        for i in range(len(self.ring)):
94            pos = (idx + i) % len(self.ring)
95            node_id = self.ring[pos][1]
96
97            if node_id not in seen:
98                result.append(node_id)
99                seen.add(node_id)
100
101                if len(result) == count:
102                    break
103
104        return result
105
106    def get_distribution(self) -> dict:
107        """Get key distribution stats (for testing)"""
108        # Sample 10000 keys and count distribution
109        distribution = {node: 0 for node in self.nodes}
110
111        for i in range(10000):
112            key = f"key_{i}"
113            node = self.get_node(key)
114            if node:
115                distribution[node] += 1
116
117        return distribution

Java Implementation:

1// Java - Consistent Hashing with Virtual Nodes
2import java.security.MessageDigest;
3import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
4import java.util.*;
5
6class ConsistentHashing {
7    private final int virtualNodes;
8    private final TreeMap<Long, String> ring; // Hash -> Node
9    private final Set<String> nodes;
10    private final MessageDigest md;
11
12    /**
13     * Constructor
14     * time = O(1)
15     * space = O(1)
16     */
17    public ConsistentHashing(int virtualNodes) {
18        this.virtualNodes = virtualNodes;
19        this.ring = new TreeMap<>();
20        this.nodes = new HashSet<>();
21        try {
22            this.md = MessageDigest.getInstance("MD5");
23        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
24            throw new RuntimeException(e);
25        }
26    }
27
28    private long hash(String key) {
29        md.reset();
30        byte[] digest = md.digest(key.getBytes());
31        long hash = 0;
32        for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
33            hash = (hash << 8) | (digest[i] & 0xFF);
34        }
35        return hash;
36    }
37
38    /**
39     * Add node with virtual nodes
40     * time = O(V log N)
41     * space = O(V)
42     */
43    public void addNode(String nodeId) {
44        if (nodes.contains(nodeId)) return;
45
46        nodes.add(nodeId);
47
48        // Add virtual nodes
49        for (int i = 0; i < virtualNodes; i++) {
50            String virtualKey = nodeId + ":vnode" + i;
51            long hash = hash(virtualKey);
52            ring.put(hash, nodeId);
53        }
54    }
55
56    /**
57     * Remove node and its virtual nodes
58     * time = O(V log N)
59     * space = O(1)
60     */
61    public void removeNode(String nodeId) {
62        if (!nodes.contains(nodeId)) return;
63
64        nodes.remove(nodeId);
65
66        // Remove all virtual nodes
67        ring.entrySet().removeIf(entry -> entry.getValue().equals(nodeId));
68    }
69
70    /**
71     * Find node for key
72     * time = O(log N)
73     * space = O(1)
74     */
75    public String getNode(String key) {
76        if (ring.isEmpty()) return null;
77
78        long hash = hash(key);
79
80        // Find first entry >= hash (ceiling)
81        Map.Entry<Long, String> entry = ring.ceilingEntry(hash);
82
83        // Wrap around if null
84        if (entry == null) {
85            entry = ring.firstEntry();
86        }
87
88        return entry.getValue();
89    }
90
91    /**
92     * Get multiple nodes for replication
93     * time = O(log N + count)
94     * space = O(count)
95     */
96    public List<String> getNodes(String key, int count) {
97        if (ring.isEmpty() || count <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
98
99        long hash = hash(key);
100        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
101        Set<String> seen = new HashSet<>();
102
103        // Start from ceiling entry
104        Long startKey = ring.ceilingKey(hash);
105        if (startKey == null) startKey = ring.firstKey();
106
107        // Traverse ring
108        Iterator<Map.Entry<Long, String>> iter = ring.tailMap(startKey).entrySet().iterator();
109
110        while (seen.size() < count) {
111            if (!iter.hasNext()) {
112                iter = ring.entrySet().iterator();
113            }
114
115            String nodeId = iter.next().getValue();
116            if (!seen.contains(nodeId)) {
117                result.add(nodeId);
118                seen.add(nodeId);
119            }
120        }
121
122        return result;
123    }
124}

Usage Example:

1# Create consistent hashing with 150 virtual nodes per physical node
2ch = ConsistentHashing(virtual_nodes=150)
3
4# Add nodes
5ch.add_node("node1")
6ch.add_node("node2")
7ch.add_node("node3")
8
9# Get node for key
10node = ch.get_node("user_12345")  # Returns node responsible for this key
11
12# Get multiple nodes for replication
13replicas = ch.get_nodes("user_12345", count=3)  # Returns [node1, node2, node3]
14
15# Remove node - only keys on this node need remapping
16ch.remove_node("node2")
17
18# Check distribution
19distribution = ch.get_distribution()
20# {'node1': 5012, 'node2': 4988} - roughly even distribution

LeetCode Related Problems:

  • LC 535 - Encode and Decode TinyURL (hash-based mapping)
  • System design interviews: Design URL shortener, Design distributed cache

3-2) Rate Limiter - Token Bucket

Concept:

  • Control request rate using token bucket algorithm
  • Tokens: Added at fixed rate (refill_rate)
  • Bucket: Max capacity of tokens
  • Request: Consumes 1 token, rejected if insufficient
  • Use Cases: API rate limiting, traffic shaping, QoS

Token Bucket vs Leaky Bucket:

  • Token Bucket: Allows bursts up to bucket capacity
  • Leaky Bucket: Smooth output rate, no bursts

Implementation:

1# LC 359 - Logger Rate Limiter (simplified variant)
2# V0 - Token Bucket Rate Limiter
3import time
4from threading import Lock
5
6class TokenBucketRateLimiter:
7    """
8    Token Bucket Rate Limiter
9
10    Parameters:
11    - capacity: max tokens in bucket (burst size)
12    - refill_rate: tokens added per second
13
14    Time Complexity:
15    - allow_request: O(1)
16
17    Space: O(1) per user/key
18    """
19
20    def __init__(self, capacity: int, refill_rate: float):
21        """
22        capacity: max tokens (burst size)
23        refill_rate: tokens added per second
24        """
25        self.capacity = capacity
26        self.refill_rate = refill_rate
27        self.tokens = capacity  # Start with full bucket
28        self.last_refill = time.time()
29        self.lock = Lock()
30
31    def _refill(self):
32        """Refill tokens based on elapsed time"""
33        now = time.time()
34        elapsed = now - self.last_refill
35
36        # Add tokens based on elapsed time
37        tokens_to_add = elapsed * self.refill_rate
38        self.tokens = min(self.capacity, self.tokens + tokens_to_add)
39        self.last_refill = now
40
41    def allow_request(self, tokens: int = 1) -> bool:
42        """
43        Try to consume tokens for request
44        Returns: True if allowed, False if rate limited
45        Time: O(1)
46        """
47        with self.lock:
48            self._refill()
49
50            if self.tokens >= tokens:
51                self.tokens -= tokens
52                return True
53
54            return False
55
56    def get_available_tokens(self) -> float:
57        """Get current available tokens (for monitoring)"""
58        with self.lock:
59            self._refill()
60            return self.tokens
61
62
63# Multi-user Rate Limiter
64class MultiUserRateLimiter:
65    """
66    Rate limiter for multiple users
67    Each user has independent token bucket
68    """
69
70    def __init__(self, capacity: int, refill_rate: float):
71        self.capacity = capacity
72        self.refill_rate = refill_rate
73        self.users = {}  # user_id -> (tokens, last_refill)
74        self.lock = Lock()
75
76    def allow_request(self, user_id: str, tokens: int = 1) -> bool:
77        """
78        Check if user can make request
79        Time: O(1)
80        Space: O(U) where U = number of users
81        """
82        with self.lock:
83            now = time.time()
84
85            if user_id not in self.users:
86                # New user: initialize with full bucket
87                self.users[user_id] = [self.capacity, now]
88
89            user_tokens, last_refill = self.users[user_id]
90
91            # Refill tokens
92            elapsed = now - last_refill
93            tokens_to_add = elapsed * self.refill_rate
94            user_tokens = min(self.capacity, user_tokens + tokens_to_add)
95
96            # Try to consume
97            if user_tokens >= tokens:
98                user_tokens -= tokens
99                self.users[user_id] = [user_tokens, now]
100                return True
101
102            # Update last_refill even if rejected
103            self.users[user_id] = [user_tokens, now]
104            return False

Java Implementation:

1// Java - Token Bucket Rate Limiter
2import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
3import java.util.Map;
4
5class TokenBucketRateLimiter {
6    private final int capacity;
7    private final double refillRate; // tokens per second
8    private double tokens;
9    private long lastRefillTime;
10
11    /**
12     * Constructor
13     * capacity: max tokens (burst size)
14     * refillRate: tokens added per second
15     */
16    public TokenBucketRateLimiter(int capacity, double refillRate) {
17        this.capacity = capacity;
18        this.refillRate = refillRate;
19        this.tokens = capacity;
20        this.lastRefillTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
21    }
22
23    private synchronized void refill() {
24        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
25        double elapsed = (now - lastRefillTime) / 1000.0; // Convert to seconds
26
27        double tokensToAdd = elapsed * refillRate;
28        tokens = Math.min(capacity, tokens + tokensToAdd);
29        lastRefillTime = now;
30    }
31
32    /**
33     * Try to consume tokens
34     * time = O(1)
35     * space = O(1)
36     */
37    public synchronized boolean allowRequest(int requestTokens) {
38        refill();
39
40        if (tokens >= requestTokens) {
41            tokens -= requestTokens;
42            return true;
43        }
44
45        return false;
46    }
47
48    public synchronized double getAvailableTokens() {
49        refill();
50        return tokens;
51    }
52}
53
54// Multi-user Rate Limiter
55class MultiUserRateLimiter {
56    private final int capacity;
57    private final double refillRate;
58    private final Map<String, UserBucket> users;
59
60    private static class UserBucket {
61        double tokens;
62        long lastRefillTime;
63
64        UserBucket(double tokens, long time) {
65            this.tokens = tokens;
66            this.lastRefillTime = time;
67        }
68    }
69
70    public MultiUserRateLimiter(int capacity, double refillRate) {
71        this.capacity = capacity;
72        this.refillRate = refillRate;
73        this.users = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
74    }
75
76    /**
77     * Check if user can make request
78     * time = O(1)
79     * space = O(U) where U = users
80     */
81    public boolean allowRequest(String userId, int requestTokens) {
82        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
83
84        users.putIfAbsent(userId, new UserBucket(capacity, now));
85
86        UserBucket bucket = users.get(userId);
87
88        synchronized (bucket) {
89            // Refill
90            double elapsed = (now - bucket.lastRefillTime) / 1000.0;
91            double tokensToAdd = elapsed * refillRate;
92            bucket.tokens = Math.min(capacity, bucket.tokens + tokensToAdd);
93            bucket.lastRefillTime = now;
94
95            // Try consume
96            if (bucket.tokens >= requestTokens) {
97                bucket.tokens -= requestTokens;
98                return true;
99            }
100
101            return false;
102        }
103    }
104}

LC 359 - Logger Rate Limiter (Simplified)

1# LC 359 - Logger Rate Limiter
2class Logger:
3    """
4    Simplified rate limiter: only 1 message per key every 10 seconds
5    This is a special case of token bucket where:
6    - capacity = 1
7    - refill_rate = 1 token per 10 seconds
8    """
9
10    def __init__(self):
11        self.msg_dict = {}  # message -> last_timestamp
12
13    def shouldPrintMessage(self, timestamp: int, message: str) -> bool:
14        """
15        time = O(1)
16        space = O(M) where M = unique messages
17        """
18        if message not in self.msg_dict:
19            self.msg_dict[message] = timestamp
20            return True
21
22        if timestamp - self.msg_dict[message] >= 10:
23            self.msg_dict[message] = timestamp
24            return True
25
26        return False

Usage Example:

1# Create rate limiter: 10 requests per second, burst up to 20
2limiter = TokenBucketRateLimiter(capacity=20, refill_rate=10)
3
4# Handle requests
5for i in range(25):
6    if limiter.allow_request():
7        print(f"Request {i}: ALLOWED")
8    else:
9        print(f"Request {i}: RATE LIMITED")
10    time.sleep(0.05)  # 50ms between requests
11
12# Output:
13# Requests 0-19: ALLOWED (burst uses all tokens)
14# Requests 20-24: RATE LIMITED (no tokens left)
15# After 1 second: 10 new tokens available

3-3) Rate Limiter - Leaky Bucket

Concept:

  • Queue-based rate limiting with constant output rate
  • Queue: Stores incoming requests (max capacity)
  • Leak: Process requests at constant rate
  • Overflow: Reject requests when queue is full
  • Difference from Token Bucket: Smooths bursts, no immediate processing of burst

Implementation:

1# V0 - Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter
2import time
3from collections import deque
4from threading import Lock
5
6class LeakyBucketRateLimiter:
7    """
8    Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter
9
10    Parameters:
11    - capacity: max queue size
12    - leak_rate: requests processed per second
13
14    Time Complexity:
15    - allow_request: O(1) amortized
16
17    Space: O(capacity)
18    """
19
20    def __init__(self, capacity: int, leak_rate: float):
21        """
22        capacity: max requests in queue
23        leak_rate: requests processed per second
24        """
25        self.capacity = capacity
26        self.leak_rate = leak_rate
27        self.queue = deque()
28        self.last_leak = time.time()
29        self.lock = Lock()
30
31    def _leak(self):
32        """Process (leak) requests at constant rate"""
33        now = time.time()
34        elapsed = now - self.last_leak
35
36        # Calculate how many requests should have been processed
37        requests_to_leak = int(elapsed * self.leak_rate)
38
39        if requests_to_leak > 0:
40            # Remove processed requests from queue
41            for _ in range(min(requests_to_leak, len(self.queue))):
42                self.queue.popleft()
43
44            self.last_leak = now
45
46    def allow_request(self) -> bool:
47        """
48        Try to add request to queue
49        Returns: True if accepted, False if rejected
50        Time: O(1) amortized
51        """
52        with self.lock:
53            self._leak()
54
55            if len(self.queue) < self.capacity:
56                self.queue.append(time.time())
57                return True
58
59            return False
60
61    def get_queue_size(self) -> int:
62        """Get current queue size"""
63        with self.lock:
64            self._leak()
65            return len(self.queue)
66
67
68# Multi-user Leaky Bucket
69class MultiUserLeakyBucket:
70    """
71    Leaky bucket rate limiter for multiple users
72    """
73
74    def __init__(self, capacity: int, leak_rate: float):
75        self.capacity = capacity
76        self.leak_rate = leak_rate
77        self.users = {}  # user_id -> (queue, last_leak_time)
78        self.lock = Lock()
79
80    def allow_request(self, user_id: str) -> bool:
81        """
82        Check if user request can be added to queue
83        Time: O(1) amortized
84        Space: O(U × C) where U=users, C=capacity
85        """
86        with self.lock:
87            now = time.time()
88
89            if user_id not in self.users:
90                self.users[user_id] = (deque(), now)
91
92            queue, last_leak = self.users[user_id]
93
94            # Leak requests
95            elapsed = now - last_leak
96            requests_to_leak = int(elapsed * self.leak_rate)
97
98            if requests_to_leak > 0:
99                for _ in range(min(requests_to_leak, len(queue))):
100                    queue.popleft()
101                last_leak = now
102
103            # Try to add request
104            if len(queue) < self.capacity:
105                queue.append(now)
106                self.users[user_id] = (queue, last_leak)
107                return True
108
109            self.users[user_id] = (queue, last_leak)
110            return False

Java Implementation:

1// Java - Leaky Bucket Rate Limiter
2import java.util.*;
3import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
4
5class LeakyBucketRateLimiter {
6    private final int capacity;
7    private final double leakRate; // requests per second
8    private final Queue<Long> queue;
9    private long lastLeakTime;
10
11    public LeakyBucketRateLimiter(int capacity, double leakRate) {
12        this.capacity = capacity;
13        this.leakRate = leakRate;
14        this.queue = new LinkedList<>();
15        this.lastLeakTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
16    }
17
18    private synchronized void leak() {
19        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
20        double elapsed = (now - lastLeakTime) / 1000.0;
21
22        int requestsToLeak = (int) (elapsed * leakRate);
23
24        if (requestsToLeak > 0) {
25            for (int i = 0; i < Math.min(requestsToLeak, queue.size()); i++) {
26                queue.poll();
27            }
28            lastLeakTime = now;
29        }
30    }
31
32    /**
33     * Try to add request to queue
34     * time = O(1) amortized
35     * space = O(capacity)
36     */
37    public synchronized boolean allowRequest() {
38        leak();
39
40        if (queue.size() < capacity) {
41            queue.offer(System.currentTimeMillis());
42            return true;
43        }
44
45        return false;
46    }
47
48    public synchronized int getQueueSize() {
49        leak();
50        return queue.size();
51    }
52}

Comparison: Token Bucket vs Leaky Bucket

Feature Token Bucket Leaky Bucket
Burst Handling Allows bursts up to capacity Smooths bursts
Output Rate Variable (up to capacity) Constant (leak rate)
Use Case API rate limiting, bursty traffic Traffic shaping, smooth output
Complexity O(1) per request O(1) amortized
Memory O(1) per user O(capacity) per user

LeetCode Related:

  • LC 359 - Logger Rate Limiter
  • LC 362 - Design Hit Counter
  • LC 635 - Design Log Storage System

3-4) Load Balancing Algorithms

Concept:

  • Distribute requests across multiple servers
  • Goals: Even distribution, high availability, low latency
  • Types: Round Robin, Weighted Round Robin, Least Connections, Consistent Hashing

Implementation:

1# V0 - Load Balancer Implementations
2from typing import List, Optional
3import time
4from threading import Lock
5
6class RoundRobinLoadBalancer:
7    """
8    Round Robin Load Balancer
9    Distributes requests sequentially across servers
10
11    Time: O(1) per request
12    Space: O(N) where N = number of servers
13    """
14
15    def __init__(self, servers: List[str]):
16        self.servers = servers
17        self.current = 0
18        self.lock = Lock()
19
20    def get_server(self) -> Optional[str]:
21        """
22        Get next server in round-robin fashion
23        Time: O(1)
24        """
25        if not self.servers:
26            return None
27
28        with self.lock:
29            server = self.servers[self.current]
30            self.current = (self.current + 1) % len(self.servers)
31            return server
32
33    def add_server(self, server: str):
34        """Add new server to pool"""
35        with self.lock:
36            if server not in self.servers:
37                self.servers.append(server)
38
39    def remove_server(self, server: str):
40        """Remove server from pool"""
41        with self.lock:
42            if server in self.servers:
43                self.servers.remove(server)
44                # Adjust current index if needed
45                if self.current >= len(self.servers) and self.servers:
46                    self.current = 0
47
48
49class WeightedRoundRobinLoadBalancer:
50    """
51    Weighted Round Robin Load Balancer
52    Servers with higher weight get more requests
53
54    Example: weights = [5, 1, 1]
55    -> Server 0 gets 5/7 of requests
56    -> Server 1 and 2 each get 1/7 of requests
57
58    Time: O(1) per request
59    Space: O(sum of weights)
60    """
61
62    def __init__(self, servers: List[str], weights: List[int]):
63        """
64        servers: list of server IDs
65        weights: corresponding weights (must be positive)
66        """
67        if len(servers) != len(weights):
68            raise ValueError("Servers and weights must have same length")
69
70        self.servers = []
71        # Expand servers based on weights
72        for server, weight in zip(servers, weights):
73            self.servers.extend([server] * weight)
74
75        self.current = 0
76        self.lock = Lock()
77
78    def get_server(self) -> Optional[str]:
79        """
80        Get next server based on weights
81        Time: O(1)
82        """
83        if not self.servers:
84            return None
85
86        with self.lock:
87            server = self.servers[self.current]
88            self.current = (self.current + 1) % len(self.servers)
89            return server
90
91
92class LeastConnectionsLoadBalancer:
93    """
94    Least Connections Load Balancer
95    Routes to server with fewest active connections
96
97    Time: O(N) per request (find min)
98    Space: O(N)
99
100    Better for long-lived connections (WebSockets, DB connections)
101    """
102
103    def __init__(self, servers: List[str]):
104        self.connections = {server: 0 for server in servers}
105        self.lock = Lock()
106
107    def get_server(self) -> Optional[str]:
108        """
109        Get server with least connections
110        Time: O(N) where N = servers
111        """
112        if not self.connections:
113            return None
114
115        with self.lock:
116            # Find server with minimum connections
117            server = min(self.connections, key=self.connections.get)
118            self.connections[server] += 1
119            return server
120
121    def release_connection(self, server: str):
122        """
123        Release connection from server
124        Call this when request completes
125        """
126        with self.lock:
127            if server in self.connections:
128                self.connections[server] = max(0, self.connections[server] - 1)
129
130    def add_server(self, server: str):
131        """Add new server"""
132        with self.lock:
133            if server not in self.connections:
134                self.connections[server] = 0
135
136    def remove_server(self, server: str):
137        """Remove server"""
138        with self.lock:
139            if server in self.connections:
140                del self.connections[server]
141
142
143class WeightedLeastConnectionsLoadBalancer:
144    """
145    Weighted Least Connections
146    Considers both connection count and server capacity
147
148    Score = connections / weight (lower is better)
149
150    Time: O(N) per request
151    Space: O(N)
152    """
153
154    def __init__(self, servers: List[str], weights: List[int]):
155        self.servers = servers
156        self.weights = {server: weight for server, weight in zip(servers, weights)}
157        self.connections = {server: 0 for server in servers}
158        self.lock = Lock()
159
160    def get_server(self) -> Optional[str]:
161        """
162        Get server with lowest connections/weight ratio
163        Time: O(N)
164        """
165        if not self.servers:
166            return None
167
168        with self.lock:
169            # Calculate score for each server
170            best_server = None
171            best_score = float('inf')
172
173            for server in self.servers:
174                score = self.connections[server] / self.weights[server]
175                if score < best_score:
176                    best_score = score
177                    best_server = server
178
179            self.connections[best_server] += 1
180            return best_server
181
182    def release_connection(self, server: str):
183        """Release connection"""
184        with self.lock:
185            if server in self.connections:
186                self.connections[server] = max(0, self.connections[server] - 1)

Java Implementation:

1// Java - Round Robin Load Balancer
2import java.util.*;
3import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;
4
5class RoundRobinLoadBalancer {
6    private final List<String> servers;
7    private final AtomicInteger current;
8
9    public RoundRobinLoadBalancer(List<String> servers) {
10        this.servers = new ArrayList<>(servers);
11        this.current = new AtomicInteger(0);
12    }
13
14    /**
15     * Get next server
16     * time = O(1)
17     * space = O(N)
18     */
19    public String getServer() {
20        if (servers.isEmpty()) return null;
21
22        int index = current.getAndIncrement() % servers.size();
23        return servers.get(index);
24    }
25
26    public synchronized void addServer(String server) {
27        if (!servers.contains(server)) {
28            servers.add(server);
29        }
30    }
31
32    public synchronized void removeServer(String server) {
33        servers.remove(server);
34    }
35}
36
37// Java - Least Connections Load Balancer
38class LeastConnectionsLoadBalancer {
39    private final Map<String, Integer> connections;
40
41    public LeastConnectionsLoadBalancer(List<String> servers) {
42        this.connections = new HashMap<>();
43        for (String server : servers) {
44            connections.put(server, 0);
45        }
46    }
47
48    /**
49     * Get server with least connections
50     * time = O(N)
51     * space = O(N)
52     */
53    public synchronized String getServer() {
54        if (connections.isEmpty()) return null;
55
56        return connections.entrySet().stream()
57            .min(Map.Entry.comparingByValue())
58            .map(entry -> {
59                String server = entry.getKey();
60                connections.put(server, entry.getValue() + 1);
61                return server;
62            })
63            .orElse(null);
64    }
65
66    public synchronized void releaseConnection(String server) {
67        connections.computeIfPresent(server, (k, v) -> Math.max(0, v - 1));
68    }
69}

Usage Example:

1# Round Robin
2rr_lb = RoundRobinLoadBalancer(["server1", "server2", "server3"])
3for _ in range(6):
4    print(rr_lb.get_server())
5# Output: server1, server2, server3, server1, server2, server3
6
7# Weighted Round Robin
8wrr_lb = WeightedRoundRobinLoadBalancer(
9    servers=["server1", "server2", "server3"],
10    weights=[5, 2, 1]  # server1 gets 5/8 of traffic
11)
12
13# Least Connections
14lc_lb = LeastConnectionsLoadBalancer(["server1", "server2", "server3"])
15server = lc_lb.get_server()  # Returns server with fewest connections
16# ... process request ...
17lc_lb.release_connection(server)  # Release when done

Load Balancing Algorithm Comparison:

Algorithm Time Space Best For Pros Cons
Round Robin O(1) O(N) Short requests Simple, fair Ignores server load
Weighted RR O(1) O(W) Varying capacity Respects capacity Static weights
Least Connections O(N) O(N) Long connections Dynamic load balancing Higher overhead
Consistent Hashing O(log N) O(VN) Caching, sticky sessions Minimal remapping Complex implementation

LeetCode Related:

  • System design: Design distributed cache, Design web crawler
  • LC 535 - Encode and Decode TinyURL (server selection for URL storage)

3-5) Interview Tips for System Design Patterns

Key Talking Points:

  1. Consistent Hashing:

    • “Minimizes data movement when nodes are added/removed”
    • “Virtual nodes ensure better load distribution”
    • “Used in Cassandra, DynamoDB, Memcached”

  2. Rate Limiting:

    • Token Bucket: “Allows controlled bursts, good for APIs”
    • Leaky Bucket: “Smooth output, good for traffic shaping”
    • “Choose based on whether bursts are acceptable”

  3. Load Balancing:

    • Round Robin: “Simple, works well for homogeneous servers”
    • Weighted: “Use when servers have different capacities”
    • Least Connections: “Better for long-lived connections”
    • Consistent Hashing: “Use for stateful services (caching)”

Common Interview Questions:

  1. “Design a rate limiter for an API” → Token bucket for burst support, Redis for distributed state

  2. “How would you distribute load across servers?” → Start with Round Robin, mention alternatives based on requirements

  3. “Design a distributed cache” → Consistent hashing for key distribution, discuss replication

  4. “How to handle server failures in load balancer?” → Health checks, automatic removal, retry logic

Implementation Tips:

  • Thread Safety: Use locks or atomic operations for concurrent access
  • Distributed Systems: Use Redis/Memcached for shared state
  • Monitoring: Track metrics (request rate, server load, error rate)
  • Health Checks: Periodically verify server availability
  • Graceful Degradation: Handle failures without complete outage
← Back to Cheat Sheets Edit on GitHub →
← Concurrency PatternsDfs →

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
